As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The decomposing body of a young woman is found in the bathroom of a New York hotel, having been undisturbed for three days. When Avery Ryan's and her team investigate, they discover the victim seemed to have been ordering room service three days after her death, and her messages to staff had released vicious malware that eradicated several hours of surveillance footage from the hotel's security cameras. The chain of evidence leads Ryan to a hacker thought to have been on-site at the time of the woman's death, but as Nelson and Ramirez continue digging for clues, it becomes clear this case is far from closed. Patricia Arquette stars(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart(n)

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Further clues lead to the controversial world of cryopreservation - the practice of keeping clinically dead patients frozen to extend their life cycle. Sasha is determined they don't put a foot wrong - but with the sobering possibility that two perpetrators are still at large, the detectives are forced to go out on a limb(n)

This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise

All evidence initially points to Xavier having crashed on one of the most dangerous sections of the course, but Jack suspects it has more to do with the discovery of a piece of fabric torn from a cycling shirt. However, one of the riders is keeping a sensitive secret. Whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon, with Nina Wadia(n)

programme.title: Question Time

Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society undertakes a period romance, a classic tale of family, duty and love across the class divide. A set built to replicate a grand country home is soon to be rocked by a literal and metaphorical storm. Sadly, the literal storm involves a water effect that threatens to flood the set, while a horse turns out to be more of a danger to body parts than expected, gunshot effects occur seemingly at random and a ceiling fan threatens to decapitate a table of diners. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: While convalescing at home from a serious operation, Gary finds himself constantly disrupted by deliveries for neighbour Stuart. Mum Denise insinuates that foul play may be afoot and that Stuart may be doing the whole thing to wind him up. Meanwhile, Terri's life is turned upside down when an old raving picture of her is shared on Facebook and she goes on a mission to get the perpetrator to take it down. Comedy, starring Tom Davis and Laura Checkley, with Simon Day, Camille Coduri and Romesh Ranganathan(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Robert Downey Jr and Emma Thompson, stars of the big-screen fantasy adventure Dolittle, while Hugh Laurie discusses his role in The Personal History of David Copperfield. Monty Python's Terry Gilliam, director of such movies as The Man Who Killed Don Quixote, also guests, while American singer-songwriter Sara Bareilles performs She Used to Be Mine, from her musical Waitress. Plus, another bunch of intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The host tests the queens' sewing skills and fashion sense as they are challenged to create show-stopping outfits from junk found at a car boot sale. Original queen of British fashion Twiggy joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel as a third queen sashays away from the competition. Plus, Drag Race All Star Raven makes a cheeky guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ken Hom and Ching-He Huang, and special guest Fay Ripley faces her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The volunteers are short on time for cooking and exercise, so Tom offers solutions to keep them on track, including quick and delicious recipes, and tips for planning ahead. For the first recipe, Tom makes a pot of beef mince - the starting point for some of the nation's favourite dishes. He uses the batch to make a cottage pie, while the rest is frozen to be used later, perhaps for chilli con carne, spaghetti bolognese or lasagne(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Brentford v Leicester City (Kick-off 12.45pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the fourth-round contest, which takes place at Griffin Park, as the Championship Bees take on Brendan Rodgers' Foxes. The home side reached the fifth round last season, before being beaten 4-1 by second-tier rivals Swansea City at the Liberty Stadium. A goal from Emiliano Marcondes was enough to defeat Stoke City here in the third round earlier this month. Leicester suffered the ignominy of losing to League Two's Newport County in round three last year, and Rodgers will be keen to avoid another upset in this game. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Dion Dublin(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Action from day six of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured the latest third-round matches. Eventual men's singles champion Novak Djokovic made it through to the last 16 with a four-set victory over Denis Shapovalov at this stage last year, while Japan's Naomi Osaka defeated Hsieh Su-wei 5-7, 6-4, 6-1 en route to her maiden Australian Open title(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a supermodel, who apparently vanished into thin air when the train she was travelling on went through a tunnel. Elen Rhys stars(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the fourth and final week of auditions, as the Dance Captains fight to take their team of three acts through to the first live show. The dancers need to impress at least 75 per cent of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition. Who will make it through to the next round?(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special sports edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty is betrayed by a man he thought he had fallen in love with, and an injured pilot who had been tasered for attempting to hijack a plane is been brought into the emergency department for treatment. Meanwhile, Archie and Connie bond over their work, and Charlie questions his decision to put Duffy in a care home(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including Hull City v Chelsea at the KCOM Stadium and West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion at London Stadium. The Tigers had a dreadful record against the Blues, having lost their last seven meetings in all competitions, including a 4-0 thumping at Stamford Bridge in the fifth round of the 2017/18 FA Cup. Plus, action from games including Brentford v Leicester City at Griffin Park, Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor, Millwall v Sheffield United at the Den, Northampton Town v Derby County at the PTS Academy Stadium, and Queens Park Rangers v Sheffield Wednesday at the Kiyan Prince Foundation Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell present action from the Conference Championship games - Kansas City Chiefs v Tennessee Titans and San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore look for bargains in Scotland, where they enjoy a very soggy Highland Games, before an auction back across the border in Clitheroe(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hugh tries to sell his families award winning cheese at farmers markets in Bath, while the Kimber family sell their meat and dairy products straight off the farm(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: There are more new arrivals at the home, meaning Jimmy and Jenny Desmond now have 28 rescued chimpanzees to look after, including an eight-week-old baby called Gaia. Plans are coming together for the new sanctuary in the forest, and the orphans learn to master tools so they can forage for food. But a deadly cold breaks out among the babies, with disastrous consequences. The team also rescues an adult male chimp called Jonny who brings a fresh set of challenges. The pressure is on to move the chimps to their new home before things get out of hand(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the ladies singles final. Julie Forrest of Scotland prevailed against Guernsey's 2011 champion Alison Merrien in last year's final. With commentary from David Corkill, John Price and Sian Honnor(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Compilation edition revisiting some of the historic venues from the series and offering some new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin visits Rodmarton Manor, an extraordinary Arts and Crafts house in the Cotswolds(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's journey through eastern England takes him to the Snape Maltings music and arts venue, the Norfolk seaside resort of Great Yarmouth and the city of Norwich(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Shappi Khorsandi and Konnie Huq search the antiques shops of Sussex in the hope of making a profit, with expert guidance from Tim Medhust and Charles Hanson. Charles is determined to impress Shappi but a fishy buy could see them flounder at the north London auction, while Konnie is put through her paces with some tough haggling for an Arts and Crafts oak armchair(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: New arrivals keep arriving at the chimpanzee orphanage in Liberia, bringing Jimmy and Jenny Desmond and their team to breaking point as they find themselves providing around-the-clock care for 38 rescued babies. After losing one of the chimps to illness, they are more determined than ever to build them a sanctuary in the forest where they can live like wild chimps. To prepare for the move, the orphans are learning to make their own nests and get their first taste of life in the forest(n)

programme.title: Travels in Euroland with Ed Balls
programme.desc: The former MP heads to Europe to discover how the divisions exposed by Brexit are reflected across the EU, exploring the role that national identity and pride have had to play in fuelling the rise of right-wing populism across the continent. He begins in the Netherlands, and the fishing towns of Den Helder and Scheveningen, where he meets a veteran fisherman who explains how EU rules and regulations have made the job harder(n)

programme.title: Mum
programme.desc: In the finale series, Cathy and her family decamp to the English countryside to celebrate Derek's birthday. This time, the family are recovering from a party the night before, and as Cathy nurses a hangover, she learns that she and Michael were less than discreet in front of the others. Subtle comedy, starring Lesley Manville and Peter Mullan(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Chris Packham: 7.7 Billion People and Counting
programme.desc: The naturalist investigates the impact of a growing human population on the world in light of predictions that the number of humans could reach 10 billion by the year 2050. He considers the steps that will need to be taken for Earth's natural resources to support so many people, visiting a city in Brazil that is on the verge of running out of water and a community school in Nigeria that may present a possible solution. Featuring contributions from leading environmental experts including David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents action from the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the ladies singles final and the concluding open singles second-round matches. With commentary from David Corkill, John Price and Sian Honnor(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Competition is fierce as the ring-netters of Newlyn go on the hunt for sardines. Also known as pilchards, they were once a staple of the Cornish fishing industry, caught using drift nets by the tens-of-thousands, then packed in salt and sent to Italy. But demand waned and the drift net fishery, once the mainstay of the Cornish fleet, faded away in the 1960s. In recent years, the humble pilchard has been rebranded as the Cornish sardine, and the hundreds of drift netters have been replaced by just 15 hi-tech ring-netters - and the sardine is once more a highly-prized and lucrative catch(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working on a cattle ranch in Austin, Texas, where he gets to grips with farm machinery and faces one of his biggest fears, horses(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth continue their profit-making challenge along the highways and byways of Derbyshire and Yorkshire. Along the way, they visit a couple of venerable survivors from the Industrial Revolution and enjoy more pastry products, before heading off to auction in the North Yorkshire town of Bedale(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: British White cattle farmers Vivien, a retired GP, and David and Angela hope to help keep their rare breeds alive as they compete at the 104th Tendring Show in Essex. Tendring is their local show and it means a lot to them and the future of their beloved herds(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: It's Knockout Week, and with only ten chefs remaining in the competition, the first group of hopefuls are set two daunting challenges by Monica and Marcus. In the brand-new Pop Up Challenge, the chefs have to show off their versatility and style, creating and serving a dish that will win over the judges and the specially-invited guests. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the chefs are pitted against each other in a cook-off to secure a place in the Semi-Finals(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the open singles quarter-finals. Eventual champion Stewart Anderson overcame top seed and 2018 title-winner Mark Dawes at this stage last year. With commentary from David Corkill and John Price(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and James Lewis scour a crowd at Ushaw in Co Durham to find objects to take off to auction. Catherine comes across a toy speed boat in its original box and James discovers a rare poster that came from a morgue which shows the average weight of adult organs. Host Paul Martin looks at some rare Catholic artefacts that survived the English Reformation(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo completes his rail journey through 1930s Britain, beginning in Norfolk at the headquarters of an international horse welfare organisation. In Peterborough, he visits a tidal defence barrier that helped save the city from flooding, while in Lincolnshire crosses the point at which the Mallard broke the speed record for a steam-powered locomotive in 1938. After a spell in the coastal town of Skegness, Michael's journey ends at RAF Coningsby where he sees some iconic Second World War aircraft, before witnessing a dazzling display featuring the RAF's latest combat aircraft(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Peter Andre and Joe Pasquale travel around Kent in search of antiques, assisted by experts Paul Laidlaw and Catherine Southon. Joe persuades expert Catherine Southon to buy a pommel horse, while Paul Laidlaw impresses his celebrity sidekick Peter with a pressed poppy from the battlefields of the First World War(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The crews race to save a teenage girl being swept out to sea on an inflatable dinghy off the packed beach at Blackpool, while among the holidaying crowds on the miles of long sandy beaches at Rhyl in north Wales, two girls get caught in a deadly current while swimming just a few metres from shore. On the Cornish coast, a woman trying out the extreme sport of coasteering for the very first time falls several metres down a cliff face onto a beach with a fast incoming tide(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The host explores the scenery of the west coast of the United States, beginning in Arizona where he learns about cacti and marvels at the sight of them in the desert and in private gardens where they have replaced traditional plants and lawns. In Palm Springs, he views gardens with famous owners, from film stars to big businessmen and politicians. Los Angeles is Monty's next stop where he hears how schoolchildren are taking up gardening. His trip ends in Seattle where he visits a tropical getaway for one of the city's main workforces and views some beautiful green spaces just outside the city(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'Quills', inviting Jimmy Carr, Tom Allen and Lou Sanders to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Olga Koch, Nigel Ng and Suzi Ruffell and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another. First shown in November 2019(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to influential writer and director Armando Iannucci, as his take on Charles Dickens's David Copperfield is released in cinemas and his new space tourism comedy series Avenue 5 launches on HBO(n)

programme.title: Lady Macbeth (2016)
programme.desc: A young woman newly married to a rich industrialist in 19th-century England finds herself starved of companionship - so when her husband is called away to one of his collieries, she begins a passionate and fiery affair with a young groom from the estate. Drama, starring Florence Pugh, Cosmo Jarvis and Paul Hilton(n)

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents action from the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the open singles quarter-finals. With commentary from David Corkill and John Price(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Leaked documents reveal how an impoverished country was corruptly exploited by its former ruling family. Reporter Richard Bilton uncovers the dubious contracts and loans that were used to get hold of some of the country's most valuable assets. He then follows the money trail back to the UK and discovers how one of the suspicious deals was run from an office in central London(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: It is 1903 and on the edge of Niagara Falls, something is wrong at Nikola Tesla's generator plant, where someone - or something - is sabotaging the maverick inventor's work. Has Tesla really received a message from Mars? And where does his great rival Thomas Edison fit into these events? The Doctor and her companions Yaz, Ryan and Graham must join forces with one of history's greatest minds to save both him and planet Earth. Guest starring Goran Visnjic, Robert Glenister and Anjli Mohindra(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show more clips from the natural world, including an alien-like snail with pulsating antennae and a 10-metre long animal called a pyrosome(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Chantry Primary take on Chobham Primary in the grand final of the inter-school quiz(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall travels to Indonesia in search of three reptiles to add to his list of predators, including the Komodo dragon and the potentially lethal green pit viper(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Holby City stars Rosie Marcel and Bob Barret look for bargains in the Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire flatlands, with the expert advice of David Harper and Louise Gostelow. However, the road trip is jeopardised when a flooded river blocks the way to the antiques shops - but a detour leads Bob to a beloved location, where he learns about landscape gardening(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: New arrivals keep arriving at the chimpanzee orphanage in Liberia, bringing Jimmy and Jenny Desmond and their team to breaking point as they find themselves providing around-the-clock care for 38 rescued babies. After losing one of the chimps to illness, they are more determined than ever to build them a sanctuary in the forest where they can live like wild chimps. To prepare for the move, the orphans are learning to make their own nests and get their first taste of life in the forest(n)

programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank (1959)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning fact-based drama based on the famous journal of a German Jewish girl forced to go into hiding with her family from Nazis in an Amsterdam attic for two years. Starring Millie Perkins, Shelley Winters and Richard Beymer(n)

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the open singles semi-finals. Stewart Anderson and Simon Skelton were the victorious players at this stage last year, prevailing against Les Gillett and Nick Brett respectively. With commentary from David Corkill and John Price(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott are at Grittleton House in Wiltshire, where items include a valuable camera and a 19th-century tribal fighting club. Paul Martin visits a Victorian school that looks the same today as it did 100 years ago(n)

programme.title: Equator from the Air
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan completes his equatorial journey in Southeast Asia. He reveals evidence that Indonesia's capital, Jakarta, is sinking, and discovers how drones are helping to manage a dangerous conflict between elephants and farmers in Sumatra. In areas of Borneo afflicted by deforestation, he searches for orangutans at night, and helps the veterinarian team health-check orphaned apes at the rehabilitation centre in Sepilok, Malaysia. Finally, he finds out how aerial tech offers hope for endangered Siamese crocodiles(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: A look across Hawaii's varied islands, discovering how they were made, and how its wildlife thrives. The most remote island chain on Earth, its tropical shores are hard to reach. However, for the hardy creatures that have made it there, such as the waterfall-climbing fish, carnivorous caterpillar and Laysan albatross, a land of opportunity awaits. From newly formed lava fields, to lush jungles, and vibrant coral reefs, these diverse and beautiful islands have it all. As this documentary reveals, its people are looking to the future and attempting to protect and foster its flora and fauna(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and the platoon welcome the first contingent of American troops to Walmington-on-Sea. All goes well initially, but when the visiting soldiers are told to make themselves at home, they show a little too much interest in the Home Guard's girlfriends, putting Anglo-US relations under strain. Classic comedy first aired in 1973, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo returns to explore more of the US rail network, embarking on the first leg of a journey across Alaska. He begins his journey amid the snow-capped mountains of the Kenal Peninsula, where he discovers that 150 years ago, Alaska was a Russian colony. Moving on, in Seward he feeds a rescued sea otter with a fearsome bite and in Anchorage he joins the US Arctic Warriors for parachute training(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on animals which start with a 'q', this time inviting Daliso Chaponda, Cariad Lloyd and Phill Jupitus to join regular panellist Alan Davies. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Untouchable: The Rise and Fall of Harvey Weinstein
programme.desc: The story of how the film producer acquired and deployed his power, charting how he allegedly abused women with seeming impunity throughout his career - from his early days as a music promoter to running movie company Miramax with his brother Bob. Women who came forward to accuse Weinstein of sexual harassment and assault tell their stories in detail, including Rosanna Arquette, Erika Rosenbaum and Paz De La Huerta, while the reporters who broke the story detail his ruthless attempts to preserve power, even as scandal threatened to engulf him(n)

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Slow Odyssey
programme.desc: Following the journey of three young cheetah siblings as they set out across the savannah in Namibia, with specially designed on-board cameras monitoring their progress. As they move through the acacia woodland, thorny scrub and open grassy plains they face encounters with animals such as zebras and warthogs that allow them to test their hunting skills(n)

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: The documentary follows British wildlife throughout summer, when animals flock from afar and competition for food is high. Hunters time their arrival from Africa to feast on the huge glut of flying insects, falcons chase after dragonflies, and sea urchins are forced to protect themselves from harmful UV rays. Plus, thermal cameras reveal the activities of nightjars on a Dorset heath and thunderstorms bring out hordes of hungry snails. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes(n)

programme.title: Concorde: A Supersonic Story
programme.desc: As new £19million museum Aerospace Bristol prepares to open, this documentary takes a look at the life of the most glamorous plane ever built. Rare footage tells the forgotten row between the French and British governments over the name of Concorde, which threatened to derail the whole project. Engineers, flight technicians and frequent fliers tell the supersonic story, aided by politician Michael Heseltine and actress Joan Collins, while a former passenger recalls sharing an intimate moment with the Rolling Stones. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his exploration of the origins of mankind by investigating the matter from which everything on Earth is formed. At a Hindu cremation in Nepal, he discusses the faith's philosophy of life as an eternal cycle of creation and destruction, and draws a parallel with the lives of the universe's stars - before revealing why everything on Earth was once a part of something else(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of a famously lavish five-day party held by the Shah of Iran in 1971 to celebrate 2,500 years of Persian monarchy. The event was held at a specially constructed oasis surrounded by a city of tents and attended by 60 world leaders, who enjoyed a five-course banquet and a pageant featuring thousands of soldiers dressed in historical costume. However, the decadence on display had far-reaching consequences, breeding resentment of the Shah's regime among the Iranian people(n)

programme.title: Chinese New Year: The Biggest Celebration on Earth
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ant Anstead present the final programme from Hong Kong, looking at what happens after New Year. Kate trains with a top dragon dancing troupe and discovers that not only is it a highly demanding Kung Fu based art, it is also taken very seriously as Hong Kong people sincerely embrace the tradition of lucky lions and dragons at New Year. Meanwhile, the Hairy Bikers are at Beijing's Temple Fair where they explore a tradition from imperial China of the Emperor starting the New Year by renewing his mandate of heaven(n)

programme.title: Bought with Love: The Secret History of British Art Collections
programme.desc: Helen Rosslyn concludes her exploration of impressive country houses, focusing on how collecting art reached its maturity in the 19th century when unprecedented wealth from Britain's economy encouraged enlightened, philanthropic industrialists to spend their fortunes on it. She focuses on the influence of pharmaceutical magnate Thomas Holloway, the Rothschild banking dynasty, and welsh sisters Gwendoline and Margaret Davies(n)

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox turns his attention to blue, examining how the colour was originally created from lapis lazuli, an imported precious stone found in the East, and that the bright paint it produced captivated artists in the Middle Ages. He explores the renowned Florentine painter Giotto's ground-breaking religious frescoes in the Arena Chapel in Padua, and charts how the colour has also helped painters from more recent times, including Gaugin, Van Gogh and Picasso, to depict emotional depth(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Simon Mayo present the edition first shown on February 2nd. Featuring performances by Holly Johnson, Roy Orbison, Robert Howard and Kym Mazelle, Sheena Easton, Michael Ball, Yazz, Hue and Cry, Samantha Fox, Simply Red, Roachford, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and Bobby Brown(n)

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The presenter explores how a new generation of composers transformed musical theatre by embracing more gritty, challenging subjects, from the mean streets of 1950s New York in West Side Story to the Dickensian London of British blockbuster Oliver! Neil learns the stories behind Broadway hits Fiddler on the Roof and A Chorus Line, and celebrates the ground-breaking work of composer and lyricist Stephen Sondheim. He also reveals the 'secrets' of some classic numbers with the help of performers Robert Lindsay and Frances Ruffelle(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mike Read and Sybil Ruscoe present the edition first shown on February 9th. Featuring performances by Samantha Fox, Rick Astley, Yazz, Hue and Cry, Def Leppard, Poison, Texas, Michael Ball, Bobby Brown, Morrissey, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and Mike and the Mechanics(n)

programme.title: Stewart Copeland's Adventures in Music
programme.desc: The performer continues his mission to understand how music works by exploring its extraordinary power to tell stories. His journey includes meetings with film director Francis Ford Coppola, who explains how simple musical devices transformed the most famous scenes in The Godfather and Apocalypse Now, Sting, who describes the secrets of his song-writing process, rapper Talib Kweli, who looks at the power of music in protest, and the French-Cuban Ibeyi sisters, who show how music has kept the Yoruban tradition alive(n)

programme.title: Boy George's 1970s: Save Me from Suburbia
programme.desc: The BBC's My Generation season continues as Boy George revisits his teenage years. While history may remember the 1970s as being marked by strikes, poverty and civil discord, the singer reveals that - for the young George O'Dowd - the decade was the beginning of his musical, social and sexual coming-of-age. He discusses how sexual repression and disenfranchisement, along with the advent of glam rock, disco and punk, gave rise to the flamboyant persona he would later unleash on the world, via Culture Club's memorable first appearance on Top of the Pops in 1982(n)

programme.title: Reginald D Hunter's Songs of the South
programme.desc: The Georgia-born, London-based comedian goes on a road trip to explore the American South through its songs both past and present, beginning with the music of Kentucky and Tennessee. He examines the disturbing tradition of blackface minstrels, and visits Dollywood theme park, a slave plantation, the home of country music in Nashville, a moonshine distillery in Gatlinburg and a string-band festival in Mount Air. Featuring contributions by Dolly Parton, the Handsome Family and Del McCoury(n)

programme.title: The Story of I Will Survive
programme.desc: The background to the hit disco song, written by lyricist Dino Fekaris after he was sacked from Motown Records. Singer Gloria Gaynor, whose career was dramatically revived by the track, returns to notorious club Studio 54 where it was first played. There's also a look at the song's most unusual cover versions - including efforts in Japanese, Hindi and on a Wurlitzer organ(n)

programme.title: Stewart Copeland's Adventures in Music
programme.desc: The performer continues his mission to understand how music works by exploring its extraordinary power to tell stories. His journey includes meetings with film director Francis Ford Coppola, who explains how simple musical devices transformed the most famous scenes in The Godfather and Apocalypse Now, Sting, who describes the secrets of his song-writing process, rapper Talib Kweli, who looks at the power of music in protest, and the French-Cuban Ibeyi sisters, who show how music has kept the Yoruban tradition alive(n)

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The presenter explores how a new generation of composers transformed musical theatre by embracing more gritty, challenging subjects, from the mean streets of 1950s New York in West Side Story to the Dickensian London of British blockbuster Oliver! Neil learns the stories behind Broadway hits Fiddler on the Roof and A Chorus Line, and celebrates the ground-breaking work of composer and lyricist Stephen Sondheim. He also reveals the 'secrets' of some classic numbers with the help of performers Robert Lindsay and Frances Ruffelle(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: After being colonised by humans, Puerto Rico's rich natural resources were rapidly exploited for agriculture, industry and urban development. This documentary follows a conservation team fighting to save the most endangered creatures from extinction after they have been pushed to the edge of existence by human encroachment. David Attenborough narrates(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop hitches a ride with the Thames river police to the new London Gateway, where she gets an overview of Britain's trade with the world from the vantage point of a giant crane. From Ramsgate, Kent, she embarks on one of the Dunkirk 'little ships' to discover the vital role it played during the Second World War, before making her way to Dover where she finds a magnificent Roman lighthouse that has guarded the shores for centuries. Plus, Neil Oliver reveals the stories of trade and defence that characterise England's south east coast(n)

programme.title: Wisting
programme.desc: With the investigator still breathing down his neck, William is determined to clear his name, while Line helps him to chase leads that his colleagues cannot follow up. programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: There is great excitement in Saint Marie when the Tour des Antilles cycling race comes to the island but the event turns sinister when the favourite, Xavier Prince, is found dead halfway around. All evidence initially points to Xavier having crashed on one of the most dangerous sections of the course, but Jack suspects it has more to do with the discovery of a piece of fabric torn from a cycling shirt. However, one of the riders is keeping a sensitive secret. Whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon, with Nina Wadia(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Stoke Newington, Hackney, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford reveal possible flaws in the Ombudsman system, which is supposed to be a customer's last line of defence. Plus, how new banking rules introduced last year to help reimburse fraud victims might be coming to a premature end and updates on some of the biggest stories from the series in the last year(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two terraces near beautiful countryside in Prestatyn in north Wales and Darley Dale in the Peak District and a shop and flat in Folkestone in Kent are all sold under the hammer. All three properties need a lot of work and Martin Roberts, Dion Sublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new investors to hear their plans(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman follows Mwakere and Jana Mrengo from Birmingham, whose hectic lies have left them looking for a better work-life balance in New Zealand. Across one week, they see the sort of life they could lead - and afford - if they were to move there on a permanent basis(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following Ned, Sophie, Rowan and Sam Scharer as they take over as the wardens of Bardsey Island off the coast of off the Llyn Peninsula in Gwynedd, managing, renovating and caring for the island while hoping to become closer as a family in the process(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction. A rare Harry Potter Book goes under the hammer with a spell-binding estimate(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi goes to extreme measures to cope with life behind bars, Rabbi David lets Valerie down, while an elderly woman with dementia mistakenly finds herself at the Mill(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mallory and Father Brown go missing, and it is up to the gang to save them from a man hell-bent on revenge. Mark Williams and Jack Deam star in the crime drama(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Wye Valley, where he helps an entrepreneurial couple with a £650,000 budget find a dream home which allows them to build a rural business. Along the way he visits the UK's first solar aquaponics greenhouse and discovers how 21st-century technology there could be the sustainable solution to growing enough food to solve the world's food shortage(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Making a living in farming today is hard work and taking big risks can help a farm survive. Here, three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity, but only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards held in London. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth browse the emporiums of north-east England, where Angus buys an expensive pen and Steven plumps for a loving jug. They then head to the deciding auction in Dundee(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dirty Sanchez daredevil-turned-vegan chef Mathew Pritchard cooks a power-packed meal for members of the Cardiff Devils Ice hockey team, with the aim of supercharging their sticks and skates as they take on their sporting rivals(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick meets Isaac for the first time, and although things get off to a rocky start, the pair quickly get to know each other. Sonia takes matters into her own hands, Phil puts his plan into action and Sharon makes an unlikely ally(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society undertakes a period romance, a classic tale of family, duty and love across the class divide. A set built to replicate a grand country home is soon to be rocked by a literal and metaphorical storm. Sadly, the literal storm involves a water effect that threatens to flood the set, while a horse turns out to be more of a danger to body parts than expected, gunshot effects occur seemingly at random and a ceiling fan threatens to decapitate a table of diners. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: The Tuckers
programme.desc: Glyn is shopped to the social and is advised a Department of Works and Pensions inspector is being sent to his house. Nats throws a birthday party for Shaks, with Roberto and Bobby at each other's throats. Comedy, written by and starring Steve Speirs, with Robert Pugh, Alexandria Riley, Joshua McCord, Ben McGregor and Jams Thomas(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Robert Downey Jr and Emma Thompson, stars of the big-screen fantasy adventure Dolittle, while Hugh Laurie discusses his role in The Personal History of David Copperfield. Monty Python's Terry Gilliam, director of such movies as The Man Who Killed Don Quixote, also guests, while American singer-songwriter Sara Bareilles performs She Used to Be Mine, from her musical Waitress. Plus, another bunch of intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: While convalescing at home from a serious operation, Gary finds himself constantly disrupted by deliveries for neighbour Stuart. Mum Denise insinuates that foul play may be afoot and that Stuart may be doing the whole thing to wind him up. Meanwhile, Terri's life is turned upside down when an old raving picture of her is shared on Facebook and she goes on a mission to get the perpetrator to take it down. Comedy, starring Tom Davis and Laura Checkley, with Simon Day, Camille Coduri and Romesh Ranganathan(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The host tests the queens' sewing skills and fashion sense as they are challenged to create show-stopping outfits from junk found at a car boot sale. Original queen of British fashion Twiggy joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel as a third queen sashays away from the competition. Plus, Drag Race All Star Raven makes a cheeky guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ken Hom and Ching-He Huang, and special guest Fay Ripley faces her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dirty Sanchez daredevil-turned-vegan chef Mathew Pritchard cooks a power-packed meal for members of the Cardiff Devils Ice hockey team, with the aim of supercharging their sticks and skates as they take on their sporting rivals(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Brentford v Leicester City (Kick-off 12.45pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the fourth-round contest, which takes place at Griffin Park, as the Championship Bees take on Brendan Rodgers' Foxes. The home side reached the fifth round last season, before being beaten 4-1 by second-tier rivals Swansea City at the Liberty Stadium. A goal from Emiliano Marcondes was enough to defeat Stoke City here in the third round earlier this month. Leicester suffered the ignominy of losing to League Two's Newport County in round three last year, and Rodgers will be keen to avoid another upset in this game. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Dion Dublin(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Action from day six of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured the latest third-round matches. Eventual men's singles champion Novak Djokovic made it through to the last 16 with a four-set victory over Denis Shapovalov at this stage last year, while Japan's Naomi Osaka defeated Hsieh Su-wei 5-7, 6-4, 6-1 en route to her maiden Australian Open title(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a supermodel, who apparently vanished into thin air when the train she was travelling on went through a tunnel. Elen Rhys stars(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the fourth and final week of auditions, as the Dance Captains fight to take their team of three acts through to the first live show. The dancers need to impress at least 75 per cent of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition. Who will make it through to the next round?(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special sports edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty is betrayed by a man he thought he had fallen in love with, and an injured pilot who had been tasered for attempting to hijack a plane is been brought into the emergency department for treatment. Meanwhile, Archie and Connie bond over their work, and Charlie questions his decision to put Duffy in a care home(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including Hull City v Chelsea at the KCOM Stadium and West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion at London Stadium. The Tigers had a dreadful record against the Blues, having lost their last seven meetings in all competitions, including a 4-0 thumping at Stamford Bridge in the fifth round of the 2017/18 FA Cup. Plus, action from games including Brentford v Leicester City at Griffin Park, Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor, Millwall v Sheffield United at the Den, Northampton Town v Derby County at the PTS Academy Stadium, and Queens Park Rangers v Sheffield Wednesday at the Kiyan Prince Foundation Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell present action from the Conference Championship games - Kansas City Chiefs v Tennessee Titans and San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville investigate the quality of mobile phone coverage and hear about home owners stuck with mortgages they can barely afford(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a property in Stourbridge in the Midlands, a terraced house in Alkrington in Manchester and a semi-detached in Wigan in Lancashire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with a woman who planned to move to New Zealand following a painful divorce, discovering if she persuaded her second husband to join her in starting over(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: How a pickpocket prowling railway stations preying on women carrying backpacks and bags was hunted down. Plus, the thieves thwarted from taking a parcel from someone's doorstep(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt are in Lincoln to help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Sheffield, while host Eric Knowles indulges his passion for vintage cars(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie and David's bond grows, while Jimmi receives a visit from his father. Emma finds herself trapped between two feuding brothers - and their cockroaches(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing clergyman uncovers secrets, hypocrisy and murder at a finishing school for young ladies. Mystery, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a £550,000 budget look for a property in Dorset, and visits the oyster beds of Poole harbour to learn how the shellfish are farmed sustainably(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: British White cattle farmers Vivien, a retired GP, and David and Angela hope to help keep their rare breeds alive as they compete at the 104th Tendring Show in Essex. Tendring is their local show and it means a lot to them and the future of their beloved herds(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth continue their profit-making challenge along the highways and byways of Derbyshire and Yorkshire. Along the way, they visit a couple of venerable survivors from the Industrial Revolution and enjoy more pastry products, before heading off to auction in the North Yorkshire town of Bedale(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells are thrown into panic as past events come back to haunt them and with Sharon having the upper hand - what lengths will she go to, to get revenge? As the election party gets under way, everyone is concerned by the sight of a drunken Linda, leaving Mick trying desperately to get her home. Lola urges Sheree to make amends with Patrick, but he is not prepared for what she tells him, and Kat opens up to Kush about her past(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The contestants are given just three hours to produce their ultimate children's birthday cake - and as if that were not tough enough, Mary Berry reminds them she knows a thing or two about baking. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.desc: The volunteers are short on time for cooking and exercise, so Tom offers solutions to keep them on track, including quick and delicious recipes, and tips for planning ahead. He uses the batch to make a cottage pie, while the rest is frozen to be used later, perhaps for chilli con carne, spaghetti bolognese or lasagne(n)

programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth continue their profit-making challenge along the highways and byways of Derbyshire and Yorkshire. Along the way, they visit a couple of venerable survivors from the Industrial Revolution and enjoy more pastry products, before heading off to auction in the North Yorkshire town of Bedale(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells are thrown into panic as past events come back to haunt them and with Sharon having the upper hand - what lengths will she go to, to get revenge? As the election party gets under way, everyone is concerned by the sight of a drunken Linda, leaving Mick trying desperately to get her home. Lola urges Sheree to make amends with Patrick, but he is not prepared for what she tells him, and Kat opens up to Kush about her past(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The contestants are given just three hours to produce their ultimate children's birthday cake - and as if that were not tough enough, Mary Berry reminds them she knows a thing or two about baking. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: There is great excitement in Saint Marie when the Tour des Antilles cycling race comes to the island but the event turns sinister when the favourite, Xavier Prince, is found dead halfway around. All evidence initially points to Xavier having crashed on one of the most dangerous sections of the course, but Jack suspects it has more to do with the discovery of a piece of fabric torn from a cycling shirt. However, one of the riders is keeping a sensitive secret. Whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon, with Nina Wadia(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Stoke Newington, Hackney, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford reveal possible flaws in the Ombudsman system, which is supposed to be a customer's last line of defence. Plus, how new banking rules introduced last year to help reimburse fraud victims might be coming to a premature end and updates on some of the biggest stories from the series in the last year(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two terraces near beautiful countryside in Prestatyn in north Wales and Darley Dale in the Peak District and a shop and flat in Folkestone in Kent are all sold under the hammer. All three properties need a lot of work and Martin Roberts, Dion Sublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new investors to hear their plans(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman follows Mwakere and Jana Mrengo from Birmingham, whose hectic lies have left them looking for a better work-life balance in New Zealand. Across one week, they see the sort of life they could lead - and afford - if they were to move there on a permanent basis(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Conmen pose as police officers to steal thousands of pounds from elderly victims, but the ring leader makes a crucial mistake. A shoplifter dances like no one's watching, but she is wrong as security cameras are filming her every move(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction. A rare Harry Potter Book goes under the hammer with a spell-binding estimate(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi goes to extreme measures to cope with life behind bars, Rabbi David lets Valerie down, while an elderly woman with dementia mistakenly finds herself at the Mill(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mallory and Father Brown go missing, and it is up to the gang to save them from a man hell-bent on revenge. Mark Williams and Jack Deam star in the crime drama(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Wye Valley, where he helps an entrepreneurial couple with a £650,000 budget find a dream home which allows them to build a rural business. Along the way he visits the UK's first solar aquaponics greenhouse and discovers how 21st-century technology there could be the sustainable solution to growing enough food to solve the world's food shortage(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Making a living in farming today is hard work and taking big risks can help a farm survive. Here, three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity, but only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards held in London. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth browse the emporiums of north-east England, where Angus buys an expensive pen and Steven plumps for a loving jug. They then head to the deciding auction in Dundee(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Sam Quek, Great Britain 400m great Roger Black, Welsh rugby union star Gareth Anscombe and world triathlon champion Jonny Brownlee join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick meets Isaac for the first time, and although things get off to a rocky start, the pair quickly get to know each other. Sonia takes matters into her own hands, Phil puts his plan into action and Sharon makes an unlikely ally(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society undertakes a period romance, a classic tale of family, duty and love across the class divide. A set built to replicate a grand country home is soon to be rocked by a literal and metaphorical storm. Sadly, the literal storm involves a water effect that threatens to flood the set, while a horse turns out to be more of a danger to body parts than expected, gunshot effects occur seemingly at random and a ceiling fan threatens to decapitate a table of diners. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: While convalescing at home from a serious operation, Gary finds himself constantly disrupted by deliveries for neighbour Stuart. Mum Denise insinuates that foul play may be afoot and that Stuart may be doing the whole thing to wind him up. Meanwhile, Terri's life is turned upside down when an old raving picture of her is shared on Facebook and she goes on a mission to get the perpetrator to take it down. Comedy, starring Tom Davis and Laura Checkley, with Simon Day, Camille Coduri and Romesh Ranganathan(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Robert Downey Jr and Emma Thompson, stars of the big-screen fantasy adventure Dolittle, while Hugh Laurie discusses his role in The Personal History of David Copperfield. Monty Python's Terry Gilliam, director of such movies as The Man Who Killed Don Quixote, also guests, while American singer-songwriter Sara Bareilles performs She Used to Be Mine, from her musical Waitress. Plus, another bunch of intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The host tests the queens' sewing skills and fashion sense as they are challenged to create show-stopping outfits from junk found at a car boot sale. Original queen of British fashion Twiggy joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel as a third queen sashays away from the competition. Plus, Drag Race All Star Raven makes a cheeky guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ken Hom and Ching-He Huang, and special guest Fay Ripley faces her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The volunteers are short on time for cooking and exercise, so Tom offers solutions to keep them on track, including quick and delicious recipes, and tips for planning ahead. For the first recipe, Tom makes a pot of beef mince - the starting point for some of the nation's favourite dishes. He uses the batch to make a cottage pie, while the rest is frozen to be used later, perhaps for chilli con carne, spaghetti bolognese or lasagne(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Brentford v Leicester City (Kick-off 12.45pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the fourth-round contest, which takes place at Griffin Park, as the Championship Bees take on Brendan Rodgers' Foxes. The home side reached the fifth round last season, before being beaten 4-1 by second-tier rivals Swansea City at the Liberty Stadium. A goal from Emiliano Marcondes was enough to defeat Stoke City here in the third round earlier this month. Leicester suffered the ignominy of losing to League Two's Newport County in round three last year, and Rodgers will be keen to avoid another upset in this game. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Dion Dublin(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Action from day six of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured the latest third-round matches. Eventual men's singles champion Novak Djokovic made it through to the last 16 with a four-set victory over Denis Shapovalov at this stage last year, while Japan's Naomi Osaka defeated Hsieh Su-wei 5-7, 6-4, 6-1 en route to her maiden Australian Open title(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a supermodel, who apparently vanished into thin air when the train she was travelling on went through a tunnel. Elen Rhys stars(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the fourth and final week of auditions, as the Dance Captains fight to take their team of three acts through to the first live show. The dancers need to impress at least 75 per cent of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition. Who will make it through to the next round?(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special sports edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty is betrayed by a man he thought he had fallen in love with, and an injured pilot who had been tasered for attempting to hijack a plane is been brought into the emergency department for treatment. Meanwhile, Archie and Connie bond over their work, and Charlie questions his decision to put Duffy in a care home(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including Hull City v Chelsea at the KCOM Stadium and West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion at London Stadium. The Tigers had a dreadful record against the Blues, having lost their last seven meetings in all competitions, including a 4-0 thumping at Stamford Bridge in the fifth round of the 2017/18 FA Cup. Plus, action from games including Brentford v Leicester City at Griffin Park, Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor, Millwall v Sheffield United at the Den, Northampton Town v Derby County at the PTS Academy Stadium, and Queens Park Rangers v Sheffield Wednesday at the Kiyan Prince Foundation Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell present action from the Conference Championship games - Kansas City Chiefs v Tennessee Titans and San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers(n)

programme.desc: Wednesday's business in the House of Lords, including a motion on the release of prisoners and a motion on criminal justice and courts regulations, followed by consideration of Commons amendments to the EU Withdrawal Agreement Bill.(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: At a time of year when many people feel the pinch in both their wallets and their waistlines, the consumer health show returns, presented by Sian Williams and Ranj Singh. Over the series they compare 12 of the latest diets, beginning with the 16:8 intermittent fasting diet and a meal replacement shake plan. They also find out how it's possible to have a drink ruining blowing a diet, investigating some of the latest low-calorie and low-alcohol tipples on the market(n)

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Major documentary telling the inside story of the royal family and the Queen's record-breaking reign. The first episode focuses on the conflict between love and duty that faces the royals - and which has occasionally threatened to bring down the House of Windsor. Edward VIII fatefully chose love when he abdicated to marry divorcee Wallis Simpson. Charles chose duty when he married Diana. The Queen, however, has managed to find a balance, maintaining a long marriage while becoming the longest-serving monarch(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: After Will and Leonard witness a deadly and deliberate hit-and-run, Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path. The victim's wife, his employee and the two brothers are all damaged characters caught in a web of lies and betrayals - and it's not just secrets hiding within the siblings' grounds. Back at home, Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay - Will's mum Amelia seems to have found a carbon copy of his late father in her new beau. Meanwhile, a new television set lands in the vicarage - just in time for Eurovision. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the glamorous island of Santorini, beginning in one of its most exclusive hotels where she discovers how one of the Aegean's poorest islands became the playground for the rich and famous. Striking away from the bustling crowds, Julia heads inland for a walk on the wild side, hiking through a forager's paradise to the hilltop village of Pyrgos to watch the sun go down. After sampling a local delicacy made from fava beans, she heads out to sea to find out how local fishermen are joining forces with environmentalists to keep Santorini's pristine waters plastic-free(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police wonder if Fiz can shed any light on Jade's assault, Daniel panics as he takes Bertie's temperature, and Bernie tells Paul that she is moving on. Meanwhile, Tim's plan to send Charlie on her way doesn't work out as planned when he finds her working at the Rovers(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Will and Ellie go on their first date with a trip to the flicks. However, a scream from the projection booth cuts the romance short as they discover a brutal murder has taken place within the cinema. Geordie arrives to investigate and soon they've found movie star connections, oddball employees and a link to the Asian subcontinent, as a shadowy figure emerges from Jack Chapman's past and refuses to retreat without causing a stir. Cathy's mother takes the children out for the day, which ends up worrying Cathy far more than it should. Robson Green, Tom Brittney, Lauren Carse and Kacey Ainsworth star(n)

The duo also try to convince meat-loving weatherman Alex Beresford that their big bosh burger is as good as the meaty ones he loves, and for their snack hacks, they show off their recipes for nut butters(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Ben Fogle recalculates the height of England's highest mountain, Scafell Pike, with the team from the Ordnance Survey. Liz Bonnin visits Cheshire's Lyme Park to witness one of autumn's greatest natural wonders - the red deer rut. Plus, chef Gino D'Acampo invites viewers to his adopted home county of Hertfordshire to find woodland ingredients for his mushroom bruschetta(n)
